plan_status.C: ✅ Done — Web App MVP (frontend build clean)
plan_status.E: ✅ Done — Streaming Enhancement (HW transcode, ABR HLS, Trickplay)
plan_status.F: ✅ Done — Admin Operations (Activity Logging, Dashboard, Scheduler, Webhooks)
plan_status.J: ✅ Done — Netflix-style Series Grouping + URL Routing (4 phases)

project.name: Velox
project.description: Self-hosted media server like Jellyfin/Emby but lighter. Go backend + React frontend.

tooling.frontend_linter: ESLint flat config (TS + React Hooks + React Refresh)

database_schema.summary: 22 tables across 18 migrations. Media server stores: libraries, movies/episodes (logical), video files (physical) with video profile/level/fps, series/seasons, genres, people/credits, subtitles, audio tracks (with sample_rate), users, watch progress, favorites, sessions, app settings.

016_subtitle_search: app_settings used for OpenSubtitles/Podnapisi API keys
017_metadata_providers: app_settings used for OMDb/TVDB/Fanart.tv API keys

key_patterns: Logical vs Physical: media (content) ↔ media_files (file on disk)
Series chain: series → seasons → episodes → media → media_files
Unified user_data: progress + favorites + rating in 1 table (Emby pattern)
XOR polymorphic: media_genres/credits CHECK exactly one of media_id/series_id
Provider IDs: tmdb_id + imdb_id + tvdb_id indexed directly
App settings: key-value JSON store for provider API keys + admin config

metadata_providers.TMDb: Primary — movies + TV. Built-in API key, rate-limited (40req/10s token bucket).
metadata_providers.OMDb: Enrichment — IMDb rating, Rotten Tomatoes %, Metacritic score. Built-in key.
metadata_providers.TheTVDB: TV enrichment — TVDB IDs, IMDb cross-reference, series status. Built-in key.
metadata_providers.Fanart.tv: Artwork enrichment — HD clearlogos, thumbnails. Built-in key.
metadata_providers.TVmaze: TV enrichment — network, status, external ID cross-reference. Free, no key needed.
metadata_providers.NFO: Local override — NFO files take priority over TMDb search.

subtitle_providers.OpenSubtitles: ⚠️ DISABLED — requires VIP API key ($2-3/mo) for media server apps. Developer key triggers warning.
subtitle_providers.Podnapisi: XML API. No API key. Currently returning 429 (overloaded) on all requests.
subtitle_providers.Subdl: REST API. API key via admin settings. Best for TV (episode→series resolution). Primary provider.
subtitle_providers.BSPlayer: SOAP/XML API. Anonymous auth (no API key). Search by file hash + IMDb ID. Good for sync accuracy.
subtitle_providers.Subscene: Web scraper via Python (DrissionPage + Chrome). Bypasses Cloudflare. Needs Xvfb in Docker. Slowest (~30s) but large Vietnamese subtitle database. Fallback provider.

layers: WithTx() on repos for transaction-scoped operations
respondJSON wraps success in {"data": ...}, logs encode errors
architecture.scan_pipeline: Pipeline (scanner/pipeline.go) — CreateJob (sync) + RunJob (async goroutine). Stages: discover → fingerprint → probe → parse → persist.
architecture.file_watcher: watcher/watcher.go — fsnotify recursive, 5s debounce, rename tracking. Config: VELOX_FILE_WATCHER=true.
architecture.playback_strategy: Direct Play → Direct Stream → Transcode Audio → Full Transcode. Admin override via app_settings.
architecture.auth: JWT (15min access + 7-day refresh + session_id), bcrypt cost 12, atomic refresh token rotation.
architecture.subtitle_search: Multi-provider: Subdl (primary) → BSPlayer (hash-based) → Subscene (scraper fallback). OpenSubtitles disabled. Podnapisi available but often 429.
architecture.docker: Multi-stage Dockerfile: Node 22 (frontend) → Go 1.24+GOTOOLCHAIN=auto (backend) → Alpine 3.21 (runtime). Nginx serves SPA + proxies /api/. Xvfb for Subscene scraper. PUID/PGID for Synology NAS.

ports.80: nginx — frontend SPA + /api/ reverse proxy to backend

volumes./data: VELOX_DATA_DIR — database, subtitles, transcode cache, trickplay

env_vars.SUBSCENE_ENABLED: true (starts Xvfb + Chrome for subtitle scraping)

deployment.synology_notes: Intel QSV: uncomment /dev/dri device passthrough. PUID=1026, PGID=100 for default Synology user.

plans.E: Streaming Enhancement (3 phases: HW transcode, ABR HLS, Trickplay)
plans.J: Netflix-style Series Grouping + URL Routing (4 phases)

conventions.language: Vietnamese in plans, English in code/commits
